rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DHIREN DWIJEN DESAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Settlement, For Allowance Of Statutory Commissions And Attorneys Fees And Decree Of Distribution Under Intestate Succession
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service has been filed. (Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.]) (2) Paragraph 8 of the petition claims that all personal property taxes were paid. What personal property? (3) Notice must be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. (4) If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] (5) Paragraph 20 of the petition appears to be incomplete. (6) The petition does not address what actions the administrator took under her IAEA authority. [CRC 7.250(a).] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARLA JEAN CAMPODONICO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been fully cleared: (1) Petition #8 to identify the trust beneficiaries. Supplement filed 9/26/2024; however, it still does not list the trust beneficiaries. Merely attaching a copy of the devisee trust without listing all beneficiaries seems insufficient as the trust is not before the court. (2) Petition #8 needs to set forth all heirs at law, including both sides of material/paternal next of kin so that all known heirs can be properly noticed. (3) If the original still cannot be located, it is not clear from the Supplement filed 9/26/24 a. whether the original was in the testator's possession or not because the testator gave Ms. Shirley Ramos-Mohazab a copy of the will. b. Whether the testator was competent until death. "Incoherent much" c. the court will determine if the section 6124 presumption applies here. If so, whether the presumption can be revoked. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BENIA L. MEYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Benjamin Meyer Jr., the decedent's brother and sole heir, cannot be located. If he predeceased the decedent, alternate heirs (e.g., issue of the decedent's grandparents such as cousins) must be identified and receive notice, but there is no record of Benjamin's death, and no person has invoked the presumption under Probate Code section 12401 or Evidence Code section 667 to establish death of Benjamin as a missing person. The Court is to determine whether notice of this petition to Benjamin can and should be dispensed with. 2) The petitioner/proposed executor, who resides in Arizona, requests an order fixing his bond at the minimum amount of $20,000. The Court is to determine a proper amount of the proposed executor's bond in its discretion given by section 8571 & CRC 7.201(b) due to the proposed executor's nonresident status. The total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate is $73,000 ($72,959 as alleged and rounded up to nearest thousand dollars). LRSF 14.16(F) requires a nonresident personal representative's bond to protect California creditors, regardless of a bond waiver. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANE MAE SEID
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of the notice has been filed. (Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.]) (3) No proof of publication of notice has been filed. (4) No Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed. (5) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (6) The petition caption is incomplete, if the petitioner seeks full IAEA authority. (7) Item 6 of the petition does not need to be completed when issue survived. (8) Item 8 of the petition must clearly indicate who the trustee of the devisee trust is. (9) If the petitioner is the trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust must also be listed, clearly identified as such, and they must be given notice. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/26/24. Courtesy copies should be: (1) proof of service; (2) proof of publication; (3) Duties and Liabilities; (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number; and (5) a supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, addressing the missing or incorrect items in the petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TINA MEI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/26/24. Prior to review of the petition, all ancillary documents must be filed, including DE-121, DE-147, DE-147S, proof of publication, proposed order. All documents to be filed by 11/8/24.
